mnemonics.title: PEAS Components
mnemonics.content: PEAS: People Eat Apples Starting Always - Performance, Environment, Actuators, Sensors - Remember the 4 key elements of agent specification!

mnemonics.title: Agent Types Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: SIMPLE → MODEL → GOAL → UTILITY → LEARNING - Each adds more capability: Reflex (no state) → State tracking → Future planning → Preference measuring → Self-improvement!

mnemonics.title: Agent Program Components
mnemonics.content: LPCP: Learning Performance Critic Problem generator - The 4 components of learning agents!

mnemonics.title: Rational Agent Definition
mnemonics.content: RATIONAL: Rational Agent Takes Optimal Action Considering Evidence Noting Performance - Maps percepts to actions maximizing performance!

acronyms.usage: Framework to specify agent task environment characteristics
The field that studies agents and intelligent behavior
The set of all possible perception sequences an agent can experience
Components of a learning agent architecture

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box with sensors on one side (input), actuators on the other (output), with agent program inside processing information and generating actions. Show feedback loop from environment back to sensors.

Use a staircase or pyramid diagram: Simple Reflex at bottom (simplest), Model-based above it, Goal-based next, Utility-based above, Learning at top (most complex). Each level adds components on top of previous.

Draw a circular diagram with Performance Element (does actions) at center, Critic (evaluates) feeding to Learning Element (improves), which updates Performance Element. Problem Generator suggests new experiences. Environment provides percepts.

Use a table format: For each agent, list Performance measure (goal metric), Environment (where it operates), Actuators (what it can do), Sensors (what it perceives). Compare different agents like medical diagnosis system, autonomous car, vacuum cleaner.

analogies.analogy: Just as a human uses eyes (sensors) to see, ears to hear, and hands/legs (actuators) to act, an AI agent uses cameras to see, microphones to hear, and motors to act.
A thermostat is like a simple reflex agent: if temperature < X, turn on heater (condition-action rule). No memory, just reacts to current reading.
A GPS maintains internal state (current location, map) to navigate even when satellites are temporarily blocked - tracks what it can't directly observe.

analogies.concept: Utility-Based Agent as Human Decision Making
analogies.analogy: When choosing a job, humans weigh multiple factors (salary, location, growth) - this is like a utility function measuring satisfaction of each option.
A child learns from feedback (critic): praise for good behavior, correction for mistakes. Over time, the child (learning agent) improves behavior based on experience.
